findings.title: Add Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Implement a three-tier priority system for notifications to visually differentiate between urgent, normal, and low-priority alerts, ensuring critical messages are not overlooked.
assumptions: Users need to distinguish between different levels of notification urgency.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.

recommendations: Design a visual representation for each priority level.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN they are categorized by priority THEN the user can see visual indicators for urgent, normal, and low-priority notifications.

findings.title: Fix Mobile Notification Delivery Issues
findings.description: Resolve silent failures in push notifications for Android 14 users to ensure all notifications are delivered successfully.
assumptions: Silent failures are causing significant user dissatisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate the FCM token refresh issue and implement a fix.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an Android 14 user WHEN they receive a push notification THEN the notification should be delivered without failure.

findings.title: Implement Granular Notification Preferences
findings.description: Create a user preference page that allows users to opt in/out of specific notification categories, providing granular control over their notification settings.
assumptions: Users want to manage their notification preferences more effectively.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Develop a new preferences API endpoint and a React settings component.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the notification preferences page WHEN they select notification categories THEN their preferences should be saved and reflected in notification delivery.

findings.title: Implement ML-Based Notification Batching
findings.description: Develop a machine learning model to batch low-priority notifications into digest formats, optimizing delivery timing based on user engagement patterns.
assumptions: Users prefer receiving notifications in a digest format.

evidence.excerpt: Users prefer receiving low-priority notifications in a digest format.

recommendations: Train the ML model on user engagement data.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has low-priority notifications WHEN the ML model processes them THEN the user receives a digest at optimal times.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Implement changes to reduce notification delivery latency from the current p95 of 340ms to under 200ms by moving to asynchronous database writes and batch processing.
assumptions: Current latency is impacting user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement asynchronous writes and batch processing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification delivery system is updated WHEN notifications are sent THEN the p95 latency should be under 200ms.

findings.title: Address Accessibility Concerns in Notification Center
findings.description: Improve the design of the notification center to enhance accessibility for users with screen readers and colorblind individuals.
assumptions: Accessibility improvements will enhance user experience for all.

evidence.excerpt: There are significant accessibility issues reported by users.

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit and implement necessary changes.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with accessibility needs accesses the notification center WHEN they navigate through notifications THEN they should be able to do so without barriers.

findings.title: Collect User Feedback on Notification Preferences
findings.description: Implement a mechanism to gather user feedback on notification preferences and delivery effectiveness to inform future improvements.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for continuous improvement.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Create a feedback form linked to the user preferences page.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the feedback form WHEN they submit their preferences THEN their feedback should be recorded for analysis.

findings.title: Monitor Notification Delivery Performance
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting for notification delivery performance metrics to ensure compliance with latency and delivery success criteria.
assumptions: Monitoring is critical for maintaining service quality.

evidence.excerpt: The unsubscribe rate measures the percentage of users opting out of notifications.

recommendations: Implement dashboards for real-time monitoring of delivery metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN notifications are sent THEN performance metrics should be tracked and alerts triggered for any anomalies.

findings.title: Conduct User Testing for Notification Features
findings.description: Perform user testing sessions to validate the effectiveness of new notification features and gather qualitative feedback.
assumptions: User testing will provide insights into feature usability.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Schedule user testing sessions post-implementation of new features.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN new notification features are implemented WHEN user testing is conducted THEN feedback should be collected and analyzed for improvements.

findings.title: Implement Notification Delivery Feedback Loop
findings.description: Create a feedback loop mechanism to inform users about the status of their notifications (delivered, read, etc.) to enhance user engagement.
assumptions: Users want to know the status of their notifications.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Design a user interface element to display notification status.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives a notification WHEN they check the status THEN they should see whether it was delivered and read.

summary: The analysis of user insights and metrics has led to the identification of critical requirements for SmartNotify, focusing on enhancing notification prioritization, addressing mobile delivery issues, and improving user preferences and accessibility. A structured backlog has been created with clear epics and user stories, ensuring comprehensive coverage of user needs and performance metrics.
